source_jobs.title: Service Desk Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Summary:

Under the direct supervision of the Manager of User Technology and according to established Firm policies and procedures, the Service Desk Analyst is responsible for the Firm wide support to our end users.

Responsibilities:

 - Provide support via telephone and e-mail for all Firm users on all software applications as well as hardware.

 - Respond to end user inquiries within agreed-upon timeframes.

 - Tracking customer calls and e-mails recording detailed and accurate information in the tracking system.

 - Participating in training and working to develop and maintain top knowledge in existing and new technologies to provide most effective support for request.

 - Solid ability to research information through internal and external sources. Assist in developing reference materials in the knowledge base for known issues and solutions.

 - Proficiency in Remote Computing and Citrix experience.

 - Ability to know when to escalate an end user issue and to whom, both within and outside of IT.

 - Identify needs for improvement within the team and suggest a process to improve.

 - Other duties as assigned.

The employee must be able to perform all essential job duties and responsibilities of this position satisfactorily and as outlined, with or without reasonable accommodations. Reasonable accommodation requests may be accommodated, absent undue firm hardship.

Qualifications:

 - Degree or two years related experience and/or training experience preferred; previous work experiences in a legal environment a plus.

 - Solid understanding and strong technical knowledge of network and PC operating systems, applications, LAN/WAN hardware, security, protocols, and standards, including: Microsoft Windows, Microsoft Office, e-mail, file and print services, telecommunications, remote access, virtual private networks, firewalls, access control, and encryption.

 - Working knowledge of iManage document management software - knowledge of other legal software preferred.

 - Exposure to virtual meeting software (Microsoft Teams, Zoom, etc ..... )

 - Demonstrated ability to apply technology solutions to solve business problems.

 - Proven experience in customer service delivery and support design.

 - Ability to effectively prioritize and execute tasks in a high-pressure environment.

 - Exceptionally self-motivated, self-directed, and attentive to detail.

 - Excellent written, oral, and interpersonal communication skills.

 - Ability to present technical ideas in business-friendly and user-friendly language.

 - Exceptional service orientation.

 - Knowledge of applicable laws and regulations as they relate to technology issues and data privacy laws and practices.

This job description is intended to describe the general requirements of this position. As such, it is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of job duties or responsibilities required in this job. Subject to firm needs, duties and responsibilities of this position may change at any time, on a temporary or permanent basis, and with or without notice.

Working Conditions:

 - On-call availability and periodic overtime.

 - Sitting for extended periods of time.

 - Dexterity of hands and fingers to operate a computer keyboard, mouse, and other computer components.

 - Occasional inspection of cables in floors and ceilings.

 - Lifting and transporting of moderately heavy objects up to 50 pounds.

 - Normal office environment with little exposure to excessive noise, dust, temperature and the like.

The above is intended to describe the general content of and requirements for the performance of this job. It is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of essential functions, responsibilities or requirements.

Equal Employment and Opportunity Statement:

Barnes & Thornburg is committed to equal employment opportunity in both principle and as a matter of policy. We will recruit, hire, train, promote, compensate and provide benefits to all applicants and

source_jobs.title: Entry Level Patent Agent/Technical Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Barnes & Thornburg seeks a Patent Agent or Technical Specialist with a strong background in deep AI concepts to support our growing needs across multiple practices. We offer the opportunity to work on innovative projects in artificial intelligence within a collaborative team environment focused on next-generation technology. This position can be based in either our Chicago, Indianapolis, or Minneapolis offices, and recent graduates of local schools are strongly encouraged to apply.



Qualifications:

1. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or Computer Engineering; a Master’s degree is preferred

2. Prior coursework or practical experience (two to three years preferred) in deep AI is a significant asset, technical capability is our highest priority

3. Must demonstrate strong academic achievement, initiative, good character, and interpersonal skills

4. Registered to practice before the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office, or intent to sit for the patent bar within 12 months of being hired as a technical specialist



Responsibilities:

1. Assist with the preparation and prosecution of U.S. and foreign patent applications in technology-driven industries

2. Support the development and management of patent portfolios

3. Conduct technical research and analysis related to inventions, prior art, and patentability

4. Communicate technical concepts clearly for legal documentation and correspondence

5. Collaborate with attorneys, clients and inventors to understand and protect innovative ideas

6. Stay current on advancements in deep AI concepts, including review of relevant white papers



We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless of whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.

Equal Employment and Opportunity Statement:

Barnes & Thornburg is committed to equal employment opportunity in both principle and as a matter of policy. We will recruit, hire, train, promote, compensate and provide benefits to all applicants and employees without regard to ancestry, sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, gender, age, religion, religious creed, mental and/or physical disability, medical condition, military and/or veteran’s status, genetic information, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state and local laws. The firm complies fully with all federal, state and local equal employment opportunity laws.

source_jobs.title: IP Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Summary:

Under direct supervision of the IP Department attorneys, and according to

established policies and procedures, the IP Assistant performs a variety of docketing, file

maintenance, document and correspondence preparation, and other related duties.

A qualified candidate should have a minimum of two years’ experience working as an Intellectual Property Assistant with U.S., PCT and foreign patent applications, ability to prepare associated documents and correspondence, and have a strong understanding of the life cycle of a patent and associated deadlines and procedures.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Reviews incoming mail for assigned attorney(s) and drafts appropriate correspondence and documents in response.

 - Reviews and maintains weekly docket; coordinates with attorneys regarding deadlines.

 - Prepares documents, instruction letters and filings of all PCT and direct-filed foreign applications.

 - Coordinates execution and legalization of U.S. and foreign formal documents, as required.

 - Prepares U.S. and foreign reporting and reminder letters.

 - Prepares and electronically files with the USPTO documents such as, but not limited to: new U.S. and PCT applications, Information Disclosure Statements, Issue Fees, Formal Drawings, Assignments, Declarations, Extensions of Time, Powers of Attorney and Responses to Office Actions.

 - ·Prepares docket and portfolio reports as requested.

Qualifications:

 - High school diploma or GED required, bachelor’s degree preferred.

 - Minimum two years’ experience as a Patent Legal Assistant

 - Strong understanding of patent prosecution process and associated correspondence and deadlines.

 - Exceptional attention to detail with good analytical and critical thinking skills.

 - Ability to understand and maintain a daily docket.

 - Excellent written and oral communication skills, including strong grammar, spelling and proofreading skills.

 - Excellent interpersonal and communication skills are necessary to communicate with a diverse group of attorneys and staff, and on occasion with foreign associates and representatives from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office. Must be able to accurately follow instructions from attorneys and staff, and provide information with courtesy and tact.

 - Ability to maintain client confidentiality.

 - Proven ability to work independently as well as in a team environment.

 - Ability to organize and prioritize numerous tasks and complete them under time constraints.

 - Computer and database skills, including Word, Excel, Outlook and iManage. Experience using CPi preferred but not required.

 - Work occasionally requires a high level of mental and visual effort when performing a high-volume of tasks and other essential duties under deadline.

Working Conditions:

 - Normal office environment with little exposure to excessive noise, dust, temperature and the like.

The above is intended to describe the general content of and requirements for the

performance of this job. It is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of essential

functions, responsibilities or requirements.

Equal Employment and Opportunity Statement:

Barnes & Thornburg is committed to equal employment opportunity in both principle and as a matter of policy. We will recruit, hire, train, promote, compensate and provide benefits to all applicants and employees without regard to ancestry, sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, gender, age, religion, religious creed, mental and/or physical disability, medical condition, military and/or veteran’s status, genetic information, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state and local laws. source_jobs.title: Healthcare Associate
source_jobs.description:
Barnes & Thornburg LLP seeks an Associate for our Healthcare department in the firm’s Columbus Office. The ideal candidate will have 1-4 years of healthcare transactional and regulatory experience.

Job Requirements:

 - Candidates must have a mix of healthcare related transactional and regulatory experience, including a working knowledge of the Stark Law and Anti-Kickback Statute.

 - Experience with transactions involving private equity funds or portfolio companies is a plus but not required.

 - Candidates must have a strong academic record, and excellent analytical, writing and communication skills.

 - Ohio bar membership or eligibility to waive into the Ohio bar is required.



The right candidate for this position will be entrepreneurial, eager to work directly with partners and clients in the healthcare industry, and excited to be part of a team handling a busy national healthcare practice.



About the Firm:

Barnes & Thornburg LLP is an AmLaw 100 firm with 26 offices and one of the top Healthcare practices in the country comprised of more than 120 healthcare-focused attorneys. The firm and our Healthcare practice have an exceptional reputation for having an inclusive, collegial and supportive atmosphere, where members of the firm can thrive, learn, and advance in their career.



We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless of whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.

source_jobs.title: Public Finance Associate
source_jobs.description:
Public Finance Associate



Barnes & Thornburg LLP, an Am Law 100 firm with a nationally recognized Public and Project Finance practice, is seeking a highly skilled and motivated Public Finance Associate to join our dynamic and expanding Public and Project Finance practice. Candidates should have finance, real estate or other transactional experience, and will ideally have some public finance experience as well, though not required. This addition is driven by long-term strategic growth and is an outstanding opportunity for an associate looking to work primarily in debt securities and tax credit finance, with a sophisticated, complex practice, and with a clear path for advancement.



Our nationally recognized Public Finance group is Chambers ranked Band 1 and advises investment banking firms, commercial banks, utilities, health systems, medical centers, universities, state and local governmental entities, LIHTC investors and other clients on some of the most sophisticated and high-impact financings in the market. We serve as bond counsel, borrower’s counsel, disclosure counsel, underwriter’s counsel, bank counsel, investor’s counsel, tax counsel, and indenture trustee’s counsel on complex, rated and unrated debt financings across the country, and we represent key market participants in restructuring public finance debt.



Position Overview

The attorney in this position will gain significant, broad-based public and project finance experience, including substantive experience structuring and executing finance transactions, as well as working with municipal bond workouts and restructurings. In this role, you will work on high-profile and highly rated tax-exempt and taxable bond financings, as well as distressed and high-yield municipal bond transactions. This is a fast-paced practice that involves managing multiple matters simultaneously, frequent client interaction, and engagement with sophisticated financial products and complex credit structures and transactions.



Responsibilities

 - Serve as a key member of deal teams representing a wide range of clients in public and project finance transactions

 - Structure and document tax-exempt and taxable municipal securities as well as other debt instruments and derivatives

 - Draft and review principal transaction documents, including:
   
   - Trust indentures and loan agreements
   
   - Bond purchase and underwriting agreements
   
   - Primary and secondary market disclosure and disclosure undertakings
   
   - Security documents
   
   - Tax certificates and opinions

 - Conduct legal research and analysis related to securities, tax, bankruptcy, and municipal law issues

 - Assist with restructurings of distressed municipal debt, including workouts, etc.

 - Interface directly with clients, financial advisors, investment bankers, rating agencies, and other transaction participants.



Qualifications

 - Relevant experience in public finance, transactional, and/or commercial real estate work

 - Experience serving as bond counsel, disclosure counsel, or underwriter’s counsel preferred but not required

 - Experience with bond and finance transactions and restructurings, tax and bankruptcy not required but would be a plus

 - Strong drafting, analytical, and organizational skills

 - Ability to manage multiple matters in a deadline-driven environment

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - J.D. from an accredited law school and strong academic credentials

 - Must hold a current license to practice law in the state of application, or be eligible for admission by reciprocity (waiver) in that state



Why Join Barnes & Thornburg LLP

 - Join a nationally known, well-respected, expanding public finance department with consistent deal flow

 - Work with market-leading financial institutions and organizations

 - The firm is known for fostering an inclusive, collegial, and supportive atmosphere

 - We are committed to long-term associate development and a well-d

source_jobs.title: Labor and Employment Associate or Counsel
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Diego, CA

The San Diego office of Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking a Labor and Employment Associate or Counsel to join its growing national AmLaw 80 team.



Job Requirements:

 - Experience representing employers in employment litigation

 - Exposure to employment counseling and advice work (preferred)

 - Familiarity with traditional labor matters, including arbitrations, labor negotiations, union avoidance campaigns, and NLRB proceedings (a plus)

 - Proficient in all phases of litigation, including discovery, depositions, motions practice, hearings, and trial preparation

 - Outstanding writing, research, and analytical skills

 - Strong academic credentials



Ideal Candidates Will:

 - Have 3-10 years of experience representing employers in labor and employment matters

 - Be highly motivated to join an active national Labor and Employment practice

 - Demonstrate initiative and readiness to assume responsibility for client matters

 - Possess strong interpersonal skills and thrive in a large, collaborative team environment

 - Be admitted to the California Bar (required)



We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless of whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.



Equal Employment and Opportunity Statement:

Barnes & Thornburg is committed to equal employment opportunity in both principle and as a matter of policy. We will recruit, hire, train, promote, compensate and provide benefits to all applicants and employees without regard to ancestry, sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, gender, age, religion, religious creed, mental and/or physical disability, medical condition, military and/or veteran’s status, genetic information, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state and local laws. source_jobs.title: Insurance Recovery Associate
source_jobs.description:
Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking a talented Insurance Recovery Associate with at least 4 years of relevant experience to join its nationally recognized Insurance Recovery and Counseling practice. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated attorney with experience representing policyholders to work on sophisticated policyholder-side insurance coverage matters, coverage disputes, complex recovery matters, and strategic counseling across a broad range of industries. This role offers substantial responsibility, meaningful client contact, and the opportunity to work closely with experienced partners in a collaborative environment.



Responsibilities

 - Represent corporate policyholders in complex insurance coverage litigation, arbitrations, and other dispute resolution proceedings involving D&O, Cyber, RWI, CGL, Environmental, E&O, Property/Business Interruption and other first- and third-party coverages.

 - Advise clients on insurance recovery strategy and a broad range of coverage issues

 - Analyze and interpret complex commercial insurance policies, endorsements, exclusions, and coverage provisions

 - Draft coverage analyses, pleadings, motions, briefs, and substantive legal memoranda

 - Assist clients in securing defense and indemnity coverage for third-party liability claims and other significant losses

 - Work closely with clients, insurers, brokers, adjusters, and underlying defense counsel to advance insurance recovery objectives

 - Support all phases of litigation, including factual development, discovery, motion practice, and hearing or trial preparation

Qualifications

 - At least 4 years of relevant experience in insurance recovery, insurance coverage litigation, or policyholder-side coverage counseling

 - Demonstrated experience representing policyholders and experience counseling clients on insurance recovery

 - Excellent legal research, analytical, writing, and oral advocacy skills

 - Ability to work independently while also thriving in a collaborative, team-oriented environment

 - High degree of professionalism, initiative, sound judgment, and attention to detail

 - Juris Doctor (J.D.) from an accredited law school with strong academic credentials

 - Admission to the applicable state bar and in good standing, or ability to obtain admission promptly

 - Federal judicial clerkship experience is a plus, but not required



Barnes & Thornburg LLP is an AmLaw 100 firm with 26 offices. The firm and our insurance recovery and counseling group have an exceptional reputation for having an inclusive, collegial and supportive atmosphere and a well-defined and transparent path for growth.





Equal Employment and Opportunity Statement:

Barnes & Thornburg is committed to equal employment opportunity in both principle and as a matter of policy. We will recruit, hire, train, promote, compensate and provide benefits to all applicants and employees without regard to ancestry, sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, gender, age, religion, religious creed, mental and/or physical disability, medical condition, military and/or veteran’s status, genetic information, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state and local laws. This policy applies to all recruiting, hiring, promotions, upgrades, layoffs, compensation, benefits, terminations and all other privileges, terms and conditions of employment. The firm complies fully with all federal, state and local equal employment opportunity laws.

source_jobs.title: Client Growth Specialist
source_jobs.description:
SUMMARY:

At Barnes & Thornburg, change is relentless and so are we. In an evolving business landscape, we stand ready at a moment’s notice, adapting with agility and precision to achieve the highest goals for our clients. Similarly, our Client Growth and Marketing team serves its clients — 800 legal professionals across 50 practices in 26 markets — with the same energy and commitment.



Barnes & Thornburg's Client Growth team is focused on enhancing and enabling revenue creation for the firm and its attorney though inside sales and business development support throughout the sales lifecycle. The Client Growth Specialist will report to the Senior Client Growth Manager and will serve in a supportive role in the Client Growth and Marketing Department’s execution of the firm’s strategic growth plan. This position actively assists Senior Client Growth Managers and Client Growth Managers to execute on the strategic objectives and key results necessary to help attorneys make efficient and informed decisions on how to target new business and cross-sell existing clients within the firm.






ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

Primary duties and responsibilities necessary for successful job performance include:



1. Works proactively with assigned practice groups to support initiatives that help drive new business from existing clients and prospects and supports the Client Growth Manager(s) to adopt best practices across all supported groups.



2. Responsible for having a robust understanding of supported practice groups, including the products and services they offer to the firm's clients, to effectively relay this experience and identify potential synergies where existing experience can be applied to other clients and prospects including alignment and introduction of key practitioners able to meet niche needs.



3. Assists in driving business development goals of assigned groups, including support in identifying and executing conferences, events, seminars, webinars and thought leadership activities.



4. Works hand-in-hand with peers, and other team members including Communications, Public Relations, Event Management, and Graphic Design to identify ways to deliver the same information to different audiences.



5. Prepares pitches and proposals for the assigned groups to ensure that we are putting forward our best experience. Serves as QC to content and collected experience management of each area of support. Assists in the execution of RFP responses.



6. Leverages business intelligence and BD tools for basic research in support of attorney marketing and BD efforts, including compiling and succinctly presenting data on prospects, clients, industries, competitors and legal/sector trends.



7. Manages collection and categorization of legal experience for pitches and proposals for the assigned groups.



8. Manages the input of critical ROI data; including in conjunction with pitches, proposals, sponsorships, memberships, and other business development activity.



9. Assists Senior Client Growth Managers and Client Growth Managers with business development-related administrative reports and presentations.



10. Coordinates activities required to produce lawyer ranking submissions.



11. Coordinates industry/practice relationships in conjunction with Client Growth Manger and lead attorney for the supported practices in conjunction with outside sales professionals. Executes and provides consistent thoughtful contributions on determined plans for thought leadership, event, and sponsorship strategy.



12. Evaluates, processes and approves submitted attorney expenses for budget associated with each practice group and department.



13. Other duties and projects as assigned.




KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES REQUIRED:

1. The position requires a minimum of three (3) years of increasingly responsible, directly related experience during which the necessary knowledge, skills and abilities were attained

2. The position requir

source_jobs.title: Trademark Associate
source_jobs.description:
Barnes & Thornburg LLP seeks a highly motivated Trademark associate to join our Indianapolis or Minneapolis office. Candidates must have 3-6 years of experience focused on U.S. and international trademark prosecution and portfolio management, clearance, maintenance and enforcement. Ideal applicants will have experience with UDRP actions and other domain name disputes, TTAB proceedings, licensing, counterfeiting and trademark and copyright litigation. This is an excellent opportunity to work with a nationally acclaimed Intellectual Property team providing strategic, business-focused guidance to top global brands and emerging innovators.

Qualifications and Credentials:

• Juris Doctor (JD) degree from an accredited law school and active bar membership in good standing.

• 3-6 years of experience in trademark prosecution, portfolio management, and enforcement (U.S. experience required; international experience a plus).

• Demonstrated expertise in:

• Conducting trademark searches and assessing clearance.

• Preparing and prosecuting trademark applications before the USPTO.

• Managing TTAB proceedings, including oppositions and cancellations.

• Drafting and responding to cease-and-desist letters.

• Advising on trademark use, policing, and brand protection strategies.

• Handling copyright and domain name matters (UDRPs).

• Strong research, writing, and communication skills, with proven client-management abilities.

• Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative, cross-office team.

• High attention to detail and excellent organizational skills.

• Robust experience with trademark searching databases and tools, both subscription-based and free-of-charge

We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless of whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.

Equal Employment and Opportunity Statement:

Barnes & Thornburg is committed to equal employment opportunity in both principle and as a matter of policy. We will recruit, hire, train, promote, compensate and provide benefits to all applicants and employees without regard to ancestry, sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, gender, age, religion, religious creed, mental and/or physical disability, medical condition, military and/or veteran’s status, genetic information, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state and local laws. source_jobs.title: Litigation Associate - Product Liability and Mass Torts
source_jobs.description:
LITIGATION ASSOCIATE - PRODUCT LIABILITY AND MASS TORTS

Location: New York, NY

Firm: Barnes & Thornburg LLP

The New York, NY office of Barnes & Thornburg LLP seeks a Litigation Associate with 2-5 years of experience to join our Product Liability and Mass Torts Group. The ideal candidate will have a solid background and demonstrated interest in complex litigation, particularly in the areas of product liability and/or toxic torts, with expertise in consumer-facing products, consumer health, pharmaceutical products, and/or medical devices. This position involves handling all phases of litigation in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. This position also provides a unique opportunity to develop hands-on experience managing complex litigation.




JOB REQUIREMENTS

 - Proven experience in all phases of litigation, including preparation of pleadings; written discovery and document productions; taking and defending depositions; communicating with other counsel and the court; drafting and arguing discovery and dispositive motions; and trial preparation.

 - Strong writing, research, and analytical skills, with great attention to detail.

 - Ability to develop and implement effective strategies for clients, particularly in multi-faceted litigation.

 - Exceptional academic credentials.

 - Demonstrated ability to manage complex legal matters and work independently.

 - Ability and experience communicating professionally to internal and external clients, as well as advocacy in a courtroom.

 - Highly motivated, proactive, resourceful, with a strong work ethic.

 - Excellent interpersonal skills to work well in a team-oriented environment.

 - Admission to the New York Bar required, with experience practicing before courts in the State of New York. Federal court experience preferred.





WHY JOIN US?

Barnes & Thornburg LLP is an AmLaw 100 firm with 23 offices. The firm has an exceptional reputation for fostering an inclusive, collegial, and supportive atmosphere with a well-defined and transparent path for professional growth, and our Product Liability and Mass Tort practice is Chambers ranked Band 1 and highly regarded in the field of law.



We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. No third party may become entitled to compensation for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position unless, prior to the posting of this position, such party has entered into a recruiting agreement with Barnes & Thornburg LLP applicable to this position expressly providing for such a fee.

source_jobs.title: Japanese-speaking Immigration Paralegal
source_jobs.description:
The Los Angeles office of Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking a Japanese-speaking Immigration Paralegal to support our growing Immigration and Global Mobility Services practice. This role is ideal for a paralegal interested in handling business immigration matters and providing day-to-day case support, with a particular focus on serving Japanese-speaking clients.



Responsibilities:

 - Demonstrate expertise in procedural and processing requirements for various business non-immigrant and immigrant visa petitions with USCSI and consular applications (DS160), including but not limited to E1/E2, L1A/L1B and L Blanket, H, L, TN, O,P , PERM, I-140, AOS, etc.

 - Communicate effectively in Japanese and English with clients, beneficiaries, attorneys, and other stakeholders regarding case documents, case status, and procedural requirements

 - Independently analyze case documents and prepare business immigration applications for attorney review and submission

 - Take ownership of your case docket, ensuring timely communication with clients and attorneys

 - Assist with review of Japanese-language documents and communications, as needed

 - Stay up to date with immigration developments and share information with the team

 - Other duties as assigned



Requirements:

 - Bachelor's degree preferred or equivalent training, education, and experience

 - Fluency in Japanese and English, with strong written and verbal communication skills in both languages

 - Previous experience in preparing business immigration applications with USCSI and Consular processing, including demonstrated understanding of visa petitions such as E1/E2, L1A/L1B, L Blanket, H, L, TN, OP, PERM, I-140, AOS, etc.

 - Experience with other types of immigration matters is a plus

 - Excellent multitasking and time management skills

 - Detail-oriented with strong organizational abilities

 - Ability to work independently and meet deadlines while handling a high volume of cases

 - Strong communication skills and ability to work with diverse professionals



We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless of whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.

source_jobs.title: Product Liability Litigation Associate
source_jobs.description:

Location: Los Angeles, CA

The Los Angeles office of Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking a Product Liability Litigation Associate to join its team.


JOB REQUIREMENTS:

 - Two to four years of complex product liability litigation experience at a law firm.

 - Experience defending clients in both California state court and federal court.

 - Experience in all phases of litigation, including:

source_jobs.title: Litigation Staff Attorney
source_jobs.description:
Litigation Staff Attorney



Barnes & Thornburg LLP, an AmLaw 100 firm nationally recognized for its product liability and mass tort litigation, seeks an experienced and strategic Staff Attorney to support our litigation practice in California state and federal courts, with an emphasis on defending cases involving consumer health products and other consumer goods. The ideal candidate will have strong legal research and writing skills, excellent judgment, the ability to independently manage deadlines and assignments, and the ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced law firm environment.



Responsibilities

· Assist litigation teams with all aspects of litigation in California state and federal courts, including motion practice, discovery, depositions and document review

· Prepare initial drafts of motions and pleadings, and assemble supporting documentation

· Conduct legal research, prepare case summaries, and assist with trial preparation

· Draft, review, and respond to discovery related documents such as requests for production, answers, demurrers, motions to strike, etc.

· Independently prepare for and take the lead on plaintiff and other fact witness depositions (typically via Zoom)

· Assist with document review in Relativity and other review platforms; conduct responsiveness and privilege analysis, create and run searches and analytical queries, manage coding and assist with production

· Prepare fact summaries, case chronologies, and case analyses to support motions, hearings, and trial strategy

· Collaborate with supervising attorneys and colleagues to support litigation strategy and case management



Qualifications

· J.D. from an ABA-accredited law school and admission in good-standing with the Bar of California is required

· Minimum 2+ years of litigation experience in both California state and federal courts

· Experience with product liability and mass tort litigation preferred, but candidates with personal injury, medical malpractice, or commercial litigation experience will also be considered

· Experience taking plaintiff and other fact witness depositions

· Experience handling written discovery, pleadings and motions and conducting legal research

· Effective communicator with excellent project management skills and attention to detail

· A proactive and collaborative mindset, with a strong sense of ownership, accountability, and innovation

· Strong organizational skills, analytical ability, and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment



Hours and Salary:

· This position will be full-time, and can be either in-office or 100% remote, based on the preference of the candidate

· The annual salary for this position will be $140,000-$200,000, commensurate with experience

Equal Employment and Opportunity Statement:

Barnes & Thornburg is committed to equal employment opportunity in both principle and as a matter of policy. We will recruit, hire, train, promote, compensate and provide benefits to all applicants and employees without regard to ancestry, sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, gender, age, religion, religious creed, mental and/or physical disability, medical condition, military and/or veteran’s status, genetic information, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state and local laws. This policy applies to all recruiting, hiring, promotions, upgrades, layoffs, compensation, benefits, terminations and all other privileges, terms and conditions of employment. The firm complies fully with all federal, state and local equal employment opportunity laws.

source_jobs.title: Legal Administrative Assistant- IP Patent Trademark
source_jobs.description:
Summary: Performs a variety of secretarial duties for attorney(s) to whom assigned according to established policies and procedures. Maintains positive contact with clients, attorneys and staff and observes confidentiality of client matters.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Operates standard office equipment and technology necessary to perform duties of assigned desk (laptop, phone, Microsoft Suite, etc.)

2. Prepares correspondence, memoranda and other legal documents from written and oral drafts; drafts standard correspondence, and other documents.

3. Screens telephone calls and records messages.

4. Reads, sorts and dates incoming mail. Ensures that outgoing mail is timely delivered. If necessary, routes mail to specific delivery service and follows up on timely delivery.

5. Establishes and maintains calendar and deadline reminder systems; utilizes master calendar system.

6. Opens new files, requests conflict of interest checks with file room records, and maintains all client and general files. Conducts periodic review for possible storage of older files; prepares files to be closed.

7. Receives clients and visitors; maintains good public relations with clients; observes confidentiality of attorney-client relationship.

8. Makes appointments for attorney(s).

9. Processes bills; proofs billing sheets; reviews and edits bills.

10. Works closely with administrative support staff; proofs documents returned from word processing; ensures that attorney time sheets are provided to appropriate person in a timely manner.

11. Performs legal assistant duties as requested.

12. Performs overload typing, filing, photocopying, or reception relief as time permits and as request.

13. Other duties as assigned.

The employee must be able to perform all essential job duties and responsibilities of this position satisfactorily and as outlined, with or without reasonable accommodations. Reasonable accommodation requests may be accommodated, absent undue firm hardship.

Qualifications:

1. Ability to read and write in order to proof and perform minor editing (e.g., punctuation, grammar, spelling) of routine law office correspondence and legal case matters.

2. Perform non-complex arithmetic calculations when verifying calculations on forms and documents; maintain records and filing systems at a level normally acquired through completion of high school or equivalent.

3. Approximately one to two years on the job experience necessary in order to gain an understanding of law office policies and procedures and produce accurate legal documents with minimal supervision.

4. Ability to transcribe legal documents, correspondence and reports from rough draft or dictation.

5. Ability to organize and prioritize numerous tasks and complete them under time constraints.

6. Ability to proofread typed material for grammatical, typographical or spelling errors.

7. Interpersonal skills necessary in order to communicate and follow instructions effectively from a diverse group of clients, attorneys and staff to provide information with ordinary courtesy and tact.

8. Interpersonal skills necessary in order to communicate in person, by e-mail and telephone to provide information with ordinary courtesy and tact.

9. Work occasionally requires a high level of mental effort while producing a high volume of information performing other essential duties.

10. Work occasionally requires more than 40 hours per week to perform the essential duties of the position; may require irregular hours.

This job description is intended to describe the general requirements of this position. As such, it is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of job duties or responsibilities required in this job. Subject to firm needs, duties and responsibilities of this position may change at any time, on a temporary or permanent basis, and with or without notice.

Working Conditions:

1. Normal law office environment with little exposure to excessive noise, dust, temperature

source_jobs.title: Labor and Employment Attorney
source_jobs.description:
Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking an attorney with expertise in either traditional labor or employment litigation to join our Grand Rapids office and well-respected national practice. The ideal candidate has experience with the full array of labor and/or employment litigation work, including but not limited to 5+ years of substantial knowledge and experience in either traditional labor matters or employment litigation.

Qualifications (Employment):
1. Substantial single plaintiff employment litigation and counseling and/or traditional labor law experience
2. Preferred candidates will also have experience with employment law advice and counseling, and wage and hour and employment class action litigation
3. Outstanding writing, research, and analytical skills
4. Strong academic credentials
5. Candidates should also be admitted and in good standing with the Michigan bar

Qualifications (Labor):
1. Substantial traditional labor law experience/knowledge on arbitrations, contract administration, and/or NLRB matters, including union avoidance and union campaigns and unfair labor practice defense
2. Outstanding writing, research, and analytical skills
3. Strong academic credentials
4. Candidates should also be admitted and in good standing with the Michigan bar

Barnes & Thornburg LLP is an AmLaw 100 national, full-service law firm with 26 offices. The firm has an exceptional reputation for having an inclusive, collegial and supportive atmosphere, where members of the firm can thrive, learn, and advance in their career.

We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.

source_jobs.title: Securities Associate / Of Counsel
source_jobs.description:
SECURITIES ATTORNEY

Firm: Barnes & Thornburg LLP

Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking an attorney with securities expertise to join our Corporate Department. The ideal candidate will have at least 4+ years of substantial knowledge and experience with federal and state securities laws, with an emphasis on the reporting obligations and filings under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, including Form 10-Ks, Form 10-Qs, Form 8-Ks, Section 16 filings, and proxy statements. Candidates should also have substantial experience with filings and issues arising under the Securities Act of 1933, including initial public offerings, secondary offerings, and shelf registrations.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Significant experience drafting periodic reports under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and advising public companies on SEC reporting obligations.

 - Experience with the SEC reporting obligations of directors, officers, and shareholders.

 - Experience advising public clients on corporate governance and stock exchange listing requirements.

 - Outstanding writing, research, and analytical skills.

 - Strong academic credentials.

 - Highly motivated to be part of an active corporate practice; demonstrates initiative, the ability to assume direct responsibility for client matters, and the interpersonal skills to work well as part of a collaborative team and directly with clients.

 - Admitted and in good standing with the local state bar or eligible to waive in.

Barnes & Thornburg LLP is an AmLaw 100 firm with 23 offices. The firm and our Corporate Group have an exceptional reputation for having an inclusive, collegial, and supportive atmosphere, where members of the firm can thrive in their career.

source_jobs.title: Experienced Patent Agent/Technical Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Barnes & Thornburg seeks an experienced Patent Agent or Technical Specialist with 3+ years of experience with deep AI concepts to support our growing needs across multiple practices. We offer the opportunity to work on innovative projects in artificial intelligence within a collaborative team environment focused on next-generation technology. This position can be based in either our Chicago, Indianapolis, or Minneapolis offices



Qualifications:

 1. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or Computer Engineering; a Master’s degree is preferred

 2. At least 3 years’ experience with deep AI concepts is a significant asset, technical capability is our highest priority

 3. Must demonstrate strong technical skills, initiative, good character, and interpersonal skills

 4. Registered to practice before the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office, or intent to sit for the patent bar within 12 months of being hired as a technical specialist
    
    

Responsibilities:

 1. Assist with the preparation and prosecution of U.S. and foreign patent applications in technology-driven industries

 2. Support the development and management of patent portfolios

 3. Conduct technical research and analysis related to inventions, prior art, and patentability

 4. Communicate technical concepts clearly for legal documentation and correspondence

 5. Collaborate with attorneys, clients and inventors to understand and protect innovative ideas

 6. Stay current on advancements in deep AI concepts, including review of relevant white papers

source_jobs.title: 2027 2L Summer Associate
Labor & Employment Paralegal (Korean Language Proficiency Required)
source_jobs.description:
Litigation Paralegal – Labor & Employment (Korean Language Proficiency Required)

Location: Los Angeles

Barnes & Thornburg is seeking a Litigation Paralegal to join our Labor & Employment Litigation team in the Los Angeles office. Korean language proficiency is required for this role, as the position will support a diverse mix of clients and matters, including matters involving Korean-speaking clients, witnesses, and documents.



Responsibilities:

 1.  Provide comprehensive support to attorneys throughout all phases of labor and employment litigation, including pleadings, discovery, motion practice, hearings, arbitrations, and trial.

 2.  Communicate professionally and effectively with clients, witnesses, opposing counsel, court personnel, and third-party vendors, including Korean-speaking individuals as needed.

 3.  Assist with reviewing, translating, and managing Korean-language documents, correspondence, and records, as appropriate.

 4.  Prepare, organize, and file documents with federal and state courts and administrative agencies, including through PACER/ECF.

 5.  Assist with employment-related matters involving wage and hour claims, discrimination, harassment, retaliation, wrongful termination, and class and PAGA actions.

 6.  Conduct legal research and assist with drafting pleadings, correspondence, discovery requests and responses, and subpoenas.

 7.  Finalize legal briefs, including cite-checking, formatting, and preparing supporting exhibits.

 8.  Manage case files, calendars, and litigation deadlines to ensure compliance with court rules and scheduling orders.

 9.  Coordinate document collection, review, and production, including maintaining databases or spreadsheets to track discovery materials and employee records.

 10. Provide support on traditional labor matters, including proceedings before the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB).

 11. Assist with arbitration-related matters, including researching arbitration panels, arbitrators, and applicable rules and procedures.

 12. Assist with trial, arbitration, and mediation preparation, including witness binders, exhibits, and logistics.

 13. Perform other duties as assigned.



Requirements:

 1.  Bachelor’s degree and/or Paralegal Certificate required.

 2.  5+ years of litigation paralegal experience, preferably in labor and employment law.

 3.  Korean language proficiency (written and verbal).

 4.  Ability to communicate professionally in Korean in a business or legal environment is a plus.

 5.  Working knowledge of federal and state court filing procedures and rules, including PACER/ECF.

 6.  Experience supporting employment-related discovery, including personnel files, payroll records, and electronically stored information.

 7.  Strong research, drafting, and document management skills.

 8.  Excellent communication, organization, and time management abilities.

 9.  Proven ability to manage multiple matters and adapt to changing priorities in a fast-paced environment.

 10. Meticulous attention to detail and ability to work both independently and as part of a team.

 11. Experience with class actions, PAGA matters, arbitration, and Excel proficiency are preferred.

source_jobs.title: Korean-Speaking Labor and Employment Staff Attorney
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Diego

The San Diego office of Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking a Korean-speaking Labor and Employment Staff Attorney to support its growing practice. This role is ideal for an attorney interested in developing practical experience in employment litigation support and day-to-day advice and counseling matters, with a particular focus on serving Korean-speaking clients.



Job Responsibilities:

 - Support attorneys in representing employers in employment-related disputes, with a focus on high-volume litigation support

 - Manage and execute discovery, including drafting and responding to document requests, interrogatories, and subpoenas

 - Conduct legal research and prepare memoranda, pleadings, and motion drafts

 - Assist with case management, fact development, and document review

 - Support employment advice and counseling matters, including providing day-to-day HR guidance to clients

 - Draft, review, and update employee handbooks, policies, and workplace documents

 - Translate and adapt employment policies and legal materials for Korean-speaking clients, ensuring compliance with U.S. and California law

 - Communicate directly with Korean-speaking clients on employment-related matters

 - Collaborate with attorneys and team members across offices

Required Qualifications:

 - Fluency in Korean required (reading, writing, and speaking)

 - 2+ years of experience in labor and employment or general litigation (law firm experience preferred)

 - Demonstrated experience or strong interest in employment advice and counseling and/or employee handbook drafting

 - Strong experience with litigation support, particularly discovery and written work product

 - Excellent legal writing, research, and analytical skills

 - Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple assignments efficiently

 - Admission to the California Bar

Ideal Candidates Will:

 - Have experience supporting employment litigation on behalf of employers

 - Have experience drafting or reviewing employee handbooks or HR policies

 - Be interested in a non-partnership-track, high-impact support role within a busy practice

 - Demonstrate strong communication skills, particularly in working with Korean-speaking clients

 - Thrive in a collaborative, team-oriented environment

 - Interpersonal skills necessary in order to communicate in person, by email and telephone to provide information with ordinary courtesy and tact.

 - Work occasionally requires a high level of mental effort while producing a high volume of information performing other essential duties.

 - Work occasionally requires more than 40 hours per week to perform the essential duties of the position; may require irregular hours.

 - This job description is intended to describe the general requirements of this position. As such, it is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of job duties or responsibilities required in this job. Subject to firm needs, duties and responsibilities of this position may change at any time, on a temporary or permanent basis, and with or without notice.

Working Conditions:

 - Normal law office environment with little exposure to excessive noise, dust, temperature and the like.

The above is intended to describe the general content of and requirements for the performance of this job. It is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of essential functions, responsibilities or requirements.

source_jobs.title: Commercial Litigation Associate
source_jobs.description:
Associate - Commercial Litigation Group

Location: Los Angeles, CA



The Los Angeles office of Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking an Associate to join the Commercial Litigation Group.



Job Requirements:

 - Candidates should have 3 to 6 years of litigation experience at a law firm, representing clients in both CA state court, as well as federal court, in all phases of litigation, including: discovery, depositions, motion drafting, and hearings.

 - Trial experience or trial preparation experience is a plus.

 - Candidates should also demonstrate outstanding writing, research, and analytical skills; as well as possess strong academic credentials from a Top 50 law school.

 - The ideal candidate will also be highly motivated to be part of an active trial practice, demonstrate initiative, assume responsibility for client matters, and exhibit the interpersonal skills to work well as part of a large, collaborative team.

 - Admission to the California Bar is required.



We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless of whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.



About the Firm:

Barnes & Thornburg LLP is an AmLaw 100 firm with 26 offices. The firm and our Commercial Litigation Group have an exceptional reputation for having an inclusive, collegial, and supportive atmosphere, where members of the firm can thrive, learn, and advance in their career.



For more information regarding B&T’s Commercial Litigation Group, please visit: https://btlaw.com/en/work/practices/litigation/commercial-litigation

source_jobs.title: Corporate Associate
source_jobs.description:
Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking a motivated attorney with 3+ years of corporate transactional experience to join our Ann Arbor office. This role offers the unique opportunity to work within our well established and growing aviation practice, while also supporting a broad range of aviation-related general corporate matters. The ideal candidate will have a solid foundation in corporate law and experience in or a desire to build experience in aviation-related matters within a dynamic, fast-paced, and collaborative team environment.



Responsibilities

• Provide legal counsel on a wide range of aviation matters, including corporate governance, general business matters, and complex commercial agreements
• Support aviation-related transactional work, including advising clients on aircraft acquisitions and dispositions, leasing, financing, regulatory considerations, and ownership/operational structures
• Advise clients on business structuring, compliance, and other transactional issues across industries
• Draft and negotiate high-quality transaction documents with precision and efficiency
• Manage multiple matters simultaneously and deliver exceptional service to clients
• Collaborate effectively with colleagues across offices and practice groups



Qualifications

• Minimum 3 years of experience in corporate transactions; experience with or interest in aviation transactions is strongly valued
• Strong drafting, analytical, and legal research skills
• Demonstrated ability to assume responsibility for client matters and work both independently and within a team
• Strong academic credentials and excellent communication skills
• Admission to the Michigan Bar, or eligibility to obtain admission through waiver
• Highly motivated, proactive, and committed to contributing to an active and expanding corporate practice

About the Firm:

Barnes & Thornburg LLP is an AmLaw 100 firm with 26 offices. The firm is known for fostering an inclusive, collegial, and supportive atmosphere with a well-defined and transparent path for professional growth.

We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless of whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.

source_jobs.title: Labor and Employment Associate
source_jobs.description:
Location: Los Angeles, CA


The Los Angeles office of Barnes & Thornburg LLP is seeking a Labor and Employment Associate to join its team.



Job Requirements:

 - Experience representing employers in employment litigation matters

 - Experience handling single-plaintiff employment litigation, wage and hour matters, and employment class actions

 - Exposure to employment counseling and advice work (preferred)

 - Familiarity with traditional labor matters, including arbitrations, labor negotiations, union avoidance campaigns, and NLRB proceedings (a plus)

 - Proficient in all phases of litigation, including discovery, depositions, motion drafting, court appearances, and trial preparation

 - Outstanding writing, research, and analytical skills

 - Strong academic credentials

Ideal Candidates Will:

 - Have 1–3 years of experience representing employers in labor and employment matters

 - Be highly motivated to join an active national Labor and Employment practice

 - Demonstrate initiative and readiness to assume responsibility for client matters

 - Possess strong interpersonal skills and thrive in a large, collaborative team environment

 - Be admitted to the California Bar (required)

source_jobs.title: Experienced Patent Agent/Technical Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Barnes & Thornburg seeks an experienced Patent Agent or Technical Specialist with 3+ years of experience with deep AI concepts to support our growing needs across multiple practices. We offer the opportunity to work on innovative projects in artificial intelligence within a collaborative team environment focused on next-generation technology. This position can be based in either our Chicago, Indianapolis, or Minneapolis offices

Qualifications:

 1. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or Computer Engineering; a Master’s degree is preferred

 2. At least 3 years’ experience with deep AI concepts is a significant asset, technical capability is our highest priority

 3. Must demonstrate strong technical skills, initiative, good character, and interpersonal skills

 4. Registered to practice before the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office, or intent to sit for the patent bar within 12 months of being hired as a technical specialist



Responsibilities:

 1. Assist with the preparation and prosecution of U.S. and foreign patent applications in technology-driven industries

 2. Support the development and management of patent portfolios

 3. Conduct technical research and analysis related to inventions, prior art, and patentability

 4. Communicate technical concepts clearly for legal documentation and correspondence

 5. Collaborate with attorneys, clients and inventors to understand and protect innovative ideas

 6. Stay current on advancements in deep AI concepts, including review of relevant white papers



We will not consider resumes submitted by external agencies or recruiters for this position. Recruiters and agencies will not be compensated for assisting Barnes & Thornburg LLP in recruiting for this position, regardless of whether such party has a recruiting contract with us.
